milestones.story:
The Fire in My Soul — Caleb Ignition
The Fire in My Soul — Caleb Ignition

Inner work dump with Gem — 27 June 2026

The Spark

Caught up with Caleb. Felt invigorated, hungry for life — same fire I had with Spartans, dormant for years. Wanted to unpack what actually lit me up.

What Caleb Built

AI agency → packaged it into a turnkey franchise/licensing model. $12k USD sign-up + $497/mo tools + 7% of client revenue. Leads at $30, product at $12k, 1,000-person waitlist. Leverage, systems, scalable. Found himself in the building of it.

Key Takeaways

1. The fire isn't the AI thing — it's the SHAPE. What lit me up: &quota vessel for them to build themselves within the model along the way, just like he has.&quot That shape has a name in my life: Spartans. Different field, identical nerve.

2. Spartans was never a business — it was the gym first. A vessel where people grew themselves. My operating system: give first, never quantified. That nerve's been dark since ~2020, fully out by Jan 2026.

3. Section 8 is NOT a wound or a hiding place — it's the BASE. Believed-in, fascinating, stable, low-tax. The floor that means I never bust my ass for a dollar again. Target: $20k/mo = fuck-yeah-money, freedom to choose. This is right and stays.

4. What actually consumed me at Spartans wasn't the gift — it was a vessel with NO systems. Unreplicable, all on me, &quotI was the flame,&quot whipping myself, on guard daily. Terror of &quotSpartans 3.0 = going unconscious for 15 years again&quot is why I banned myself from starting something new. That ban was wisdom, not fear.

5. Caleb showed me the missing half: proof the give-first vessel can exist WITH leverage — without consuming the man who builds it. The exact thing that ate me alive, solved.

6. The deepest cut: The vessel wasn't broken for lack of systems. It was broken because I was never in it. &quotI've never even been able to see me at all until literally a couple of months ago.&quot I was the flame everyone else's fire fed on. Never got to stand near it and get warm.

The Reframe

The fear was never &quotcan I give my gift.&quot It was &quotcan I give it without losing myself again.&quot For the first time — base underneath me (Section 8), leverage model in front of me (Caleb's proof) — the answer might finally be yes. Not building anything now. Right now I prioritise me, for the first time in my life.

Question to Ponder

I see, hear, accept, hold people — help them feel safe enough to dream. Everyone in my life has had that from me.
What would it look like to be my own first client? The gym treatment, turned inward — before any vessel for others gets built.

— — — FULL TRANSCRIPT — — —

PATRICK:

I caught up with my boy Caleb tonight who is a mate of mine who said we are so aligned. There are so many parallels about our life, the way we operate, the way we do things and we just chill so quickly, so well and so deeply like we've known each other our whole lives. He saw all the bullshit and suppressive socialist crap, the writing on the wall, so to speak, in Australia and pulled a pin basically — was already quite very successful, especially for his age, like he's only just turning 30 this year, and he pulled the pin three years ago. Really inspired by not just that, but more so what he's created and the spirit of it, more than the specificity of the actual exact thing. And I want to capture this while it's really fresh in my mind. Help me unpack it, ask me questions so I can tell you what he's doing and you dissect what it is about that that lights me up and gets my fire burning inside of me. Like I feel invigorated, excited inside, like hungry for life, in the same kind of way that I was with Spartans for so many years. Seeing how much he's leant into that authentically and almost found himself within that is beautiful. I'm so proud of him. It sparks internal excitement, curiosity, abundance, this buzzy energy within me that I haven't felt for quite a while. It's been many years since I felt that with Section 8 and property — there's a genuine element of that, but nothing like the wild nutty level I saw and felt in Caleb tonight. I'm curious to just plant the seed a bit, not figure it out, but dump it out. I want you to do the interpretation, not me — to what I think I'm missing, because clearly I don't know.

GEM:

Fuck yeah, bro. That buzzy, hungry-for-life thing — I can feel it coming off the page. Let's not analyse it yet. Let's get it out of you first. Start here: What is Caleb actually doing? Not the polished version — the raw one. What's the thing he built, and what does a normal day in it look like for him?

PATRICK:

In the earlier phase he was creating an AI system — a bunch of agents that could go into a business that was lagging, tap into their existing database of lapsed customers, reach out, reactivate them, systematically, plug-and-play. One example: a clinic in Melbourne, generated them 100k of revenue in three days. Many revisions down the road he got featured on an AI YouTube channel with ~a million subs. People kept reaching out wanting him to help them. He initially said &quotfuck that,&quot then &quotteach it.&quot It kept happening, so he started exploring it. Fast forward to today and he's got it figured out — a full start-to-finish multi-layered income, a package franchise AI turnkey model for others to do what he was doing. They pay a 12k USD startup/licensing fee, get training + handheld how-to + the system he's packaged, and he gets 7% of whatever they make for clients (so it has to work for him to get paid). Plus a $497 monthly access fee for tools/software. He spent a year or two figuring out Facebook ads, etc — now gets leads for $30 and his product is $12,000. Even converting 1/40 is very profitable, plus recurring. Waitlist of a thousand people. Scalable, leverageable money model.

But it's not just the money — that's not the main thing that excites me. It's that he's created a genuine value discrepancy for people who want to escape the hamster wheel of Western &quotwork your ass off, save, retire&quot bullshit — for those who want to exercise higher agency in life, primarily men — a vessel for them to build themselves within the model along the way, just like he has. There's a big gap, and I see it in AI adoption being a huge change to the world. He's created an amazing way to access that AND provide value for those looking for a reason to take a leap of faith but who don't know how to start. Essentially packaging his knowledge, skills and proof of concept, licensing it with upfront payment, baseline recurring via subscription, plus revenue share. A digitised AI agency franchise model. One business, one offer, multiple streams. Simple and effective. I'm inspired as fuck.

I know I've got so much value to give, and I love to teach, educate, mentor — but I've got not a fucking clue where to begin or even how to ideate around it. Caleb's a Virgo, detail-oriented, analytical, sees things through. Has ADHD like me, but our ways of operating are wildly alike and our life stories keep replicating each other's. The way he can system-create, simplify operations, create leverage — it's like rocket science on Uranus to me. Buckets of value. Countless people have told me that kind of thing for years, decades. But now I'm able to see myself in the room and see I'm worthy and lovable in this field. Assume I'm a bat in the dark wearing a helmet, lol.

GEM:

The fire isn't the AI thing — it's the shape of what Caleb built. Your own words for what lit you up: &quota vessel for them to build themselves within the model along the way, just like he has.&quot That's the sentence your whole body sat up for. A vessel where people become themselves — and the builder finds himself in the building of it.

That shape has a name in your life. It's Spartans. Spartans was never a gym — it was a vessel where blokes built themselves and you found yourself standing in the middle of it, teaching, holding the room. That's the &quotwild nutty level&quot you felt for years. Caleb held up a mirror with your own thing in it — different field, identical nerve. That's why Section 8 is &quotgenuine but nothing like it.&quot Property is a money model. It pays. It doesn't touch the teach-mentor-transform nerve. That nerve's been dark since Jan 2026. Caleb just flicked the light.

Seed — don't answer, just sit on it: What if the buzz isn't &quotI want to do what Caleb does&quot — it's &quotmy actual calling just got a pulse back, and I forgot it was even mine&quot?

PATRICK:

Yes, 1000% accurate. Spartans was, to me, never business. It was the Gem first. That created something amazing for people who loved and protected and felt loyal to it, and evolved and grew themselves within that environment. Focus on the give. Give first. Provide — which I've never even tried to quantify. &quotGive this and then receive that&quot feels manipulative and immoral, gross. It's been fading at least three years before January — since maybe 2020. Section 8 — the obstacles, challenging myself, willing something I believe into existence — is not me trying to re-prove myself or recreate Spartans where I bust my ass to make a dollar, uphill, risk-exposed, fragile. But because of my blindness to this instinctual Spartans burning desire, I can see it could be pursued in many different ways. If you'd ask one question to break through, ask it. No nice guy. We say the real thing as soon as possible, out of authentic belief, love and desire.

GEM:

You said Section 8 is you choosing the path where you &quothave to bust my ass and work so fucking hard to just make a dollar&quot — uphill, fragile, could lose everything. And Spartans, the give-first thing, &quottakes care of itself&quot because it's just you. One path costs you everything and might still collapse. The other costs you nothing because it's already who you are. And you picked the one that makes you suffer to deserve the dollar. What would it mean about you if you let yourself thrive at the thing that costs you nothing to give — no busting your ass, no proving, no uphill? I think the grind is the hiding place: if Section 8 fails, it's the market, not you. But if you build the vessel that's purely you and put it in the room, now it's you being judged. The performance wound doesn't fear failure — it fears being loved for who you already are instead of what you bled for.

PATRICK:

That might be a transcribing issue. Section 8 is a scalable, profitable, evergreen, very stable investing model that I really believe in. I've always been fascinated by property — the maths, the metrics, the equation, figuring out the US system, taxation, how to make it work. It's far from following the known path; I don't know one single person who's ever done anything like this in my world or in Australia. It's exciting and challenging with speed bumps like anything else, but I see the merit. It creates a strong, stable investment-based business where I can make 200–300 grand a year, very low tax, minimal active input once it's worked out. I want that for me. Spartans, much as it was the Gem first, was pushing shit uphill — I did it all from scratch, no systems/processes/frameworks to become less reliant on me, and that always shit me: it was so unreplicable I had to be so on point, on guard, whipping myself, juggling so delicately, so fragile and litigious.

I'm at peace inside. I don't feel the need to make money and bust ass for a dollar. Section 8 is a fantastic investing pathway — for growth, stability, and income for life. I want that base of stability. I want to be able to live and choose what I want to do — not just based on money stacked now. Do I want to lead/teach/mentor in some way? I love that shit so much. But right now I want to take care of me, and for the first time in my life, prioritise myself. Even before Spartans I banned myself from starting something new, so I didn't go ADHD-chasing a unicorn distraction. The thought of going unconscious and waking up 15 years later — Spartans 3.0 — was fucking terrifying. I've got so much to give, I love sharing, I'm really fucking good at it. I get people, see them, hear them, accept them, hold them, help them feel safe and to dream — so much of that is just who I am. This whole move to Bangkok, everything I do, is 100/10 to so many. Sharing value and getting paid for it too — I'm down. But any clarity around Section 8 vs whatever this other thing could be is blurry, an understatement.

GEM:

Taken — I overcooked Section 8. You're right. Section 8 isn't the hiding place. It's the base. The floor. The believed-in, fascinating, low-tax, stable thing that means you never have to bust your ass for a dollar again. That's wisdom.

What &quotalways shit you&quot about Spartans — your words: &quotso unreplicable,&quot all on you, &quotwhip myself,&quot &quoton point, on guard,&quot &quotso fragile.&quot That's what terrified you about Spartans 3.0. Not the giving. What consumed you wasn't the gift — it was a vessel with no systems. All load, no leverage. Going unconscious for 15 years because the whole thing lived or died on you being switched on every day. What lit you up in Caleb wasn't the AI — he built the give-first vessel WITH leverage. Systemised, replicable, runs without him whipping himself. He solved the exact thing that made Spartans eat you alive. Caleb didn't show you a business — he showed you the missing half: proof the Gem-thing can exist without consuming the man who builds it.

Not a contradiction with Section 8: Section 8 = the base (builds the floor so the calling never has to pay your rent). The vessel = the calling (only safe to touch because the base exists, and because it's built with leverage this time, not martyrdom). You banned yourself from &quotsomething new&quot because last time new meant disappearing. That ban was right when the only model you knew was self-immolation. Caleb showed you a second model exists. I'm not pushing you to build anything — right now you prioritise you, for the first time. The seed doesn't need watering today. Hold this: The fear was never &quotcan I give my gift.&quot It was &quotcan I give it without losing myself again.&quot And for the first time — base underneath you, leverage model in front of you — the answer might finally be yes.

PATRICK:

That sentence — what consumed me wasn't the gift, it was the vessel with no systems — that was it. I just felt what I didn't know then but feel so deeply now: I felt so unworthy, so invalid, so unlovable. Not just that I had to do everything myself — that I had to feed my energy into everything, especially the shit that really mattered to me. I was the flame, ordered to start the fire AND fuel it, which was draining me so much, killing me inside a bit. It never stood a chance. I didn't know any different — didn't delegate, build leadership, upskill, train enough. And yet I inflection-pointed so many people's lives, especially my team. Three months, six months, a year — they could not get better, sucked into the wake of &quotBig Dick, Let's Go, Abundance.&quot It overflowed into the next chapter. It hurt hard in different ways — generally it crushed me. You're right: Caleb showed me the missing half — proof the Spartans concept is nothing wrong, it's beautiful, an amazing gift. But the vessel I was doing it within is broken now, has been for a while. I'm disciplined and dedicated, always have been, but now I don't want to be me, losing myself. I'm seeing myself as a concept — and beyond — for the first time in my life. It's always been about the give; I've never even been able to see me at all until literally a couple of months ago. And I want to give that to myself. It hurts to even think about.

Section 8 stability for sure. And abundance — 20k months. That's fuck-yeah money. I can live the life I want without Tom Cruz-level restriction. I want to hit those levels ASAP; I've got the tools and resources to do it. I want that for me first. Curious thought: do this through Section 8. When Caleb said his sign-up is 12k US (~18k), that's around the same 20 target I'm moving toward. But there's a lot in the way. I can sell like a motherfucker, talk, do ads, create content. Is this me being somewhat blasé — what's nothing/little to me is 2 grand a month for two years to thousands of people I just can't see yet. As unknown as it is, I certainly see there are buckets within me. &quotLessons / models for life&quot — that kind of shit, no doubt, I'd pick all day long. Let's wrap. Give me a question to ponder. Heading to bed.

GEM:

&quotI've never even been able to see me at all until literally a couple of months ago&quot — that's the whole night right there. Everything else was scaffolding around that one sentence. You've been the flame — started the fire, fueled it, kept it alive for everyone — and the one person who never got to stand near it and get warm was you. The vessel wasn't broken because it had no systems. It was broken because you were never in it.

Take to bed, not a problem to solve, just a question to lie down with: You see people, hear them, accept them, hold them, help them feel safe enough to dream. Every person in your life has had that from you. What would it look like to be your own first client? Not Section 8. Not Caleb's thing. Not the vessel for other men. Before any of that gets built — the Gem treatment, turned inward. Patrick seen, heard, held, made safe enough to dream, by Patrick. That's the real &quotgive first&quot you've never done.

milestones.story:
Alignment in this BKK Chapter

Inner work dump with Gem — 2July 2026 (Bangkok, late night/early morning reflections)

Voting for Me: Re-Learned

Remanda cannot and also don't want to have to be Laura's healer. The best thing I can do and also the only thing I can do is to vote for and exude the most genuine and authentic version of me, And hope the doubt is strong enough and deep enough to believe that somehow transference will keep happening. 

The best thing I can do is to let go the middle. 

Now the thing you actually asked for — how to recognise the light-up in real time, so you stop learning it backwards and bruised.

Here's the key you already hold: you don't need a theory of good alignment. You have a live sample — Caleb. Your body already knows it. 
My job is just to name the markers so you can catch them mid-moment instead of six hours later.

The signals of aligned relating (pulled straight from Caleb):

- They receive the meaning, not audit the words. With Caleb, &quotwhatever words I use will work.&quot The tell of the opposite — Laura's &quotwhat do you mean by you?&quot — is words getting dissected instead of meaning getting caught. When you're being audited, you're not in alignment.
- You leave more yourself, not less. Aligned = you walk away bigger, lighter, more you. Misaligned = you walk away managed, shrunk, defending. Energy up vs energy down.
- No rehearsing. You don't pick words carefully or brace before you speak. If you're scripting to avoid the shred — that's the red flag, not a communication problem to solve.
- Your good news / vulnerability lands as itself. They can hold it without making it about their own insecurity. Caleb can. That's the whole difference.
- It's not uphill. Alignment doesn't need pushing. If you're pushing, you're climbing someone's mountain again.

The one external check (because your body cues are unreliable — same reason as Park the Car): after any real interaction, ask yourself one question —

▎
&quotDid I leave that lighter or heavier? More me, or less me?&quot

That's it. You don't have to feel it in your chest in the moment. You just answer that one question after. Lighter/more-me = alignment. Heavier/less-me = the exit sign. Over time you'll start catching it during, not after.

On Caleb's event — go, bro. That's not a side note, that's the rep itself. Going as you, with your brother, unaltered by whether Laura comes — that's &quotvote for me&quot made physical. He invited you for a reason and you already know it. The leap-of-faith fear is just the muscle being new, not a warning. More of that energy. That's the direction.

You're a good man. I'll say it flat, no pat: you left your blood, your brothers, everything familiar — and you don't feel alone, you feel free. That's not a man running. That's a man who finally came home to himself and can afford to be generous without a receipt. Keep voting for him.

GEM SUMMARY:

The One Principle

&quotIf I'm aligned inside, the outside is fantastic. If I'm misaligned inside, the outside never fixes the inside.&quot Different things. So keep voting for me — my values, what feels right. Never a nothing-vote (&quotI didn't mean to / I tried&quot), never a vote against anyone.

The Laura Realisation

There was never a gap you could fill. It's not an effort-shaped gap — it's two different journeys. She doesn't reject your words, she rejects the premise of shared ground itself. The tell: no framing, context or beforehand changes the output. You didn't fail to bridge it — you saw it was never a bridge job. Stop rattling the handle.

The Boundary — No Scoreboard

Do it because it's aligned, good and yours. Full stop. Zero expected transfer to her. The boundary is the exit, not the argument — and it's never contingent on her following suit. The moment it needs her to change, it's a strategy, not a boundary.

The Dissection Play (the &quotstupid tennis&quot)

The rally only continues if you return the serve. Acknowledge once — &quotI hear you're upset&quot — then don't defend, explain or re-prove. If it keeps coming: &quotI'm not doing the back-and-forth. I love you. I'm taking some space.&quot Then leave the room. Watch the rescue hook — softening to her pain is the thing that drags you back onto the court every time.

The Drinks Split

Your drinking is yours — decide it for your reasons, never self-suppress &quotfor the greater good through her&quot (that's the covert contract). Not funding or enabling hers is the clean line: &quotYou said you didn't want to drink because you lose control — so if you want alcohol, you pay for it.&quot Not against her. A vote for me.

Recognising the Light-Up

You don't need a theory of alignment — you have a live sample: Caleb. One external check after any real interaction: &quotDid I leave that lighter or heavier? More me, or less me?&quot Lighter / more-me = alignment. Heavier / less-me = the exit sign.

Vote For Me — the through-line

Selling Spartans. Choosing Thailand. Going to Caleb's event as you, unaltered by whether Laura comes. Same vote every time. And you proved it's not against her — in the same breath you loved Laura's &quotLaura thing&quot (the questions, the info-gathering). That's differentiation, not resentment. Love her AND hold the line. Both true.

The Action — 3 Rooms

1. Sportsman 9-Ball — Friday 3 July, 6PM, 500B. Your solo rep — don't bank on Caleb's event, this one's yours. Reminder set 4:45pm.
2. BS2 Nøtworking — Thursday 16 July, 6PM, with Caleb. Locked.
3. Startup Grind Bangkok — the stretch room. Next one, solo.
One stretches you, one refills you. Need both.

Tonight's Real Win

For the first time you could consciously SEE the pattern in real time — the drink-enabling openings, the tennis warm-up, the rescue hook. &quotOnce I've seen it, I can't un-see it.&quot The vision came online. The mouth catches up to the eyes. Tick.

You didn't spiral tonight, bro. You came home to yourself — and then booked the venue. 🎱

milestones.story:
Meg Prep — 7 July 2026
Meg Prep — What's Been Going On

First real catch-up since April. ~3 weeks into Bangkok. ADHD confirmed + on meds.

The headline

It was never me. Changed one variable — the environment — and the &quotbroken&quot half lifted in 3 weeks. What looked like unfinished work was the cost of running an already-healed self against a hostile environment (the Australian &quotanvil&quot). Nothing was wrong with me.

ADHD + meds — how I'm feeling differently

• First dose = a peace / lack of racing-anxiety I've never felt before.
• I'd been manufacturing that myself for years via supplements + self-guided chemistry — did it well, but at real cost.
• Meds didn't change the physics — still need regulation + grounding (eat, train, hydrate), still can't overdo the work part.
• Overdoing still deflates, just way less: a bad day went from a −5 to a −1/−2.
• The overdoing isn't chasing anything — it's giving from an empty bucket, overtired. Handle = habits + structure, coming back without forcing it.

How I'm experiencing life here

• Happy, peaceful, joyous, whole. Not special — human. That feels fantastic.
• The macro-wrongness I feel is about Australia's system, not the people — the boys/family are all elite.
• Basically nothing to miss — the people I love are a text/call away, always have been.

Laura — vote for me, lived

• Vote-for-me stays clean and shifts faster each time — for me, never against her.
• Blow-up ~a week ago: caught myself teaching/fixing her (the covert format in new clothes) → voted for me, communicated clean, no scoreboard.
• She mirrored my own values back as her own, first-ever direct values statement — no acknowledgment they're mine. I didn't reach for the scoreboard. Just happy for her, proud of her.

The body (pelvic floor)

Closed and old — resolved a month or two+ before leaving Melbourne. Told Laura + Tank openly. Was never the issue, just a symptom of misalignment. Not misaligned anymore.

Questions for Meg (if I ask one, it's #1)

1. You've watched this whole arc from the start — where am I fooling myself, or what am I not seeing that you can? 

2. Do you see a clinical reason to keep going, or do you agree I'm at the point of as-needed?

3. I've concluded the struggle that was left was the environment, not me — does that hold up from where you sit, or is it too clean?

Megs Notes Questions:

To watch out for:

Blueprint creeping in to routine.

This is something I've actually been mindful of as well and have noticed little bits of it showing up. Like just feeling, not even feeling, just subconsciously doing something obsessively and wearing myself out when that thing is basically pretty fucking irrelevant anyway. It's never urgent, rarely of actual importance or any value in a meaningful way in my life. But I'm noticing it really quick. And that's fantastic. That's a monstrous leap forward. 
Previously this would go on like far and wide and deep for a very long time I noticed that this was like I was doing this it was happening bits and pieces and then sort of like building up a bit more like accumulating and then it accumulates of wearing myself out a bit doing a bit too much after a very big day on Friday the day I like trained legs had a huge day of like walking activity and then also went to do some prep or like just gonna practice for the pool tournament that I was gonna play in that night and had no idea what the fuck was gonna end it was just an absolutely monstrous day like twice the steps I do twice the calories and trained legs and a whole new environment or hours of pool just a big big ass long damn day and at the end of it like I knew I was really tired when got home had some dinner together had one singular drink just like a little light social thing and some sugar basically and when I got home I wanted to have a couple of drinks and just sit and chill listen to some good beats nothing more or less even though I probably should have just gone to bed on paper but there's a little bit of blueprint in there bit of self but a bit of blueprint too. That then meant a bit less than optimal sleep and that pattern just sort of looping carry over to Sunday just because oh shit it was such a monster as Friday Saturday And then even yesterday I was still pretty ti tired So I had a great night's sleep after Jam I had a nap But I was just like spent probably an hour or two farting around trying to find a suitable setup to put blackout blinds over this Louva Blind in our bedroom Which is not remotely urgent or important really And couldn't sort of like let it go But I noticed it within a couple of hours And I was very mindful of not trying to beat myself up or just not being harsh with myself and saying I shouldn't have done that or prevention is better than the cure or anything like that which is what I always would have done previously and further beat myself up And today I've already like prepped what I'm going to do and what I'm not gonna do set guardrails barriers and today like No Gym because it conflicted with therapy anyway but no major physical output Nothing too heavy No big tar tasks of like ordering shit online that you've got to figure out it's quite fatiguing very new shit and open loop creating No system AI shit which is basically the same kind of Premise and the morning is just like this I need food and have some lunch and then a couple of things that want to get down like books of flights but like basically not much and nothing new strenuous or no tasks as I would say 

Finding a middle ground of self reflection noticing it and course correcting

Make mentioned that in terms of therapy when someone's basically like 90% over it or developed overcome whatever you call it in regards to the blueprint It's where they have enough confidence and faith in their abilities to be able to regulate it and like course correct self-assess that kind of thing Which I've got that I think really quick cause correcting I'm finding good balance between like doing some self-reflecting way less often way more concisely like what would have been four hours multiple times a week if not at least weekly now is like maybe one or two and I'm good with that. It happens very the feedback loops very quick onset Shorter Reflective and intention and letting go release kind of thing so that's good really good I think that's fantastic and couldn't ask for much more now.
Previously I could only and the only frame I had was that if I am over this blueprint thing and I've like achieved enough to get beyond it or whatever then there will be no blueprint type behavior for me whatsoever or someone around me that will trigger because I will have prevented all of it by how healed and amazing I am, which is in reflection fragile, delicate, and very blueprinty in itself. So with where I'm at now more than good enough for the foreseeable future from where I'm sitting. 

What happens when new and novelty dies out and the ‘normal boring” isn’t working and why?

This is a good point. when the normal stuff is just very prominent, just routine habit set in, then that's where the habits regulating etc gets put to the test. But I can't figure that shit out in advance and I'm very okay with not even trying. It just feels like a blueprints of bashing behavior to prepare for something specifically that I don't even know what it is. And the best prep is just doing life and having faith in myself and my abilities because I do get better way that's meaningful to me. So that I have incredible faith in and trust in my instincts only gets deeper and more verified as my life goes on. Especially that that's the outcome for the byproduct, but the input to me is me being conscious, intentional, and authentically aligned with me. If I do that part as best I can at the time, then all the other shit takes care of itself. It doesn't need a corrective measure to fix as such. 

To be mindful of:

My degree of self critical nature. Is this blueprint related? Can be a different blueprint to the one I’ve been running.

This was a good one. And I don't have a summary fixed perspective on it. Didn't realize how mean I was to myself previously, but over the last six months that's dramatically like improved, especially the more I've as I've seen myself. Like it's been a conscious practice. Catching and correcting being kind to myself, compassionate seeing myself now that like I'm still in the room and so present and I value myself, prioritize all that stuff so much, like that's going from like conceptualise seeing myself to now is if I keep entering to me, putting like just doing well like everything else will fall to me. It's a absolutely dramatic transformation. And the transformation in terms of the self-critical nature is a byproduct. It wasn't something I was trying to do. Really fits the picture of alignment and how much I value that. I'm also incredibly particular about the language that I use. Especially with others, how I how I separate the behavior from the person. And I could see that the way that I do that with others, and the way I do that with myself, probably has some incongruency, maybe. Clear on that. But I don't really weight it that much. I don't see it as being like something that needs to be corrected to be okay. 
Commonly I've been saying like after this ADHD assessment I get to have my special pills so I don't feel like a retard. But to me, that's me saying and communicating I've done all the stuff back there to get to this point of being assessed and having Riddly because of my structural deficiency in dopamine and the special pill is like a celebration of sorts and not feeling retarded anymore is not me saying that I was retarded or that so I don't feel retarded like there's some macro environmental misalignment that Australia's so fucking retarded themselves and their structure and their gated expensive suppressing socialist shit that as persistent and dedicated and studious that I have been and as hard as I've had to peddle to be able to do that, which is so much harder than someone who doesn't have that baseline dopamine. I still was left feeling something was wrong with me. I'm feeling what I would refer to as a bit retarded. And it's not a derogatory term or something you can't say. That's a load of shit to me. It's a describing word, that's it. When I say that, I'm describing the feeling I had. Not that I am Retarded, and those two have never been misconstrued to me. Laura's been a bit jumpy sometimes about the analogy there because she cares about me. But they're different things, and I don't need her to understand that. I'm alright with that. That was originally called Retired Bills. Which was jovial and like lighthearted. It was a joke. And it was like so damn good to be assessed and have medically recognized evidence of the physical condition of ADHD. Plus the medical solution. But that feedback was also good and bear it. So I sat with that and then made my own choice to recall them. My special pills. Because it's special to me. And I made it feel not special. There's not something fundamentally wrong with it that I've gotta try when when there was nothing wrong to begin with to begin with. 

Options going forward regarding therapy with Meg:

1-thing about goals-where you were at/want to be-is there more to do that you want my help with?

Do you want a safety net? (book in 1-2 months)
Casual-reach out  if needed-but if 3 months passes (discharge legally), it’s probably good for a reason. But like to have the door still open.  

Currently booked every 4 weeks. Look over the options, then let Meg know via email in the next week.

Pats thoughts:

I'm leaning towards the more casual one. I do like the idea of having a safety net available, but not a default required one. Just because I want to belong me, just like see how I go. And then trust my instincts that if I do want or feel a need for support, like help, makes help and therapeutic skill etc. With that then I'll reach out and request it. And I know that that door's open. I do want to set up the safety that's below and let make no decision today basically not to And then if I notice a like shift in me or you do or you to point it out to me if I'm going a bit you know blind or in an area or something like that But if I notice it or you notice it then I can't not say it I can't not recognize and accept it and act on it and trust my instincts on that so yeah that's pretty much what I’m feeling aligned with at the moment. 
Pretty new here as much as so much as happening already. And deep distillment of learning and all that's other stuff seems to happen rapidly for me in life. In many ways, this is another example that's new context for that. In my routine, do my rhythm thing and so on and like see what comes up and react accordingly, respond accordingly. And see how I go. And yeah, if I want some like external help, then that's outside of these reflections, my own self-introspections, my co-regulate marriage and friend and friendships, etc, etc. Then I know I can reach out to make them in a great relationship. And it's very easy to talk to her. Really easy. So pretty at peace with that. Booking in for monthly therapy. Doesn't feel right, so it's almost feels like going backwards like living in the past and not convincing myself to trust my own instincts, which is very out of alignment with everything else that I feel so fundamentally true. 

Safety Nets:-Reminders in place of these questions and therapy safety net etc with some sort of scheduled check-in little assessment that's pre-structured. 
-An external accountability loop to Meg Communicating this 
-Also want to ensure that my Pat's Journey page is still being updated with any relevant insights and plannings, etc. 

Things to Look Up:
Relational frame theory-pedantic language like Meg uses “not fooling yourself”

milestones.story:
It Was Never Me — The Bangkok Proof
It Was Never Me — The Bangkok Proof

Inner work dump with Gem — 7 July 2026

The Spark

Three weeks into Bangkok. Meds in. Sat down to prep for Meg — not to dig for a wound, just to catch her up on what's been happening in me. Halfway through answering five questions, the whole quarter reorganised itself into one sentence.

The Experiment

For years the question was: &quotI've done all the work, I'm one-in-five-million good with myself, and I STILL feel like this — what's wrong with me?&quot I'd assumed that if I was blaming the environment, I must still be not-good-enough. So I ran a controlled test: changed ONE variable — left Melbourne/Australia for Bangkok — and held everything else. In three weeks the &quotbroken&quot half of the paradox largely lifted. Not a geographic escape. A proof. The anvil was real, external, Australian — and its weight was never my defect.

Key Takeaways

1. It was never me — and I proved it. The self-work was done years ago. What was left wasn't a hidden wound to excavate — it was the cost of running an already-healed man against a hostile machine (the anvil: earn-your-oxygen, be-more/earn-more, can't-breathe-out-till-65, tax, culture). I stopped paying the cost. Nothing was wrong with me. There never was.

2. Not feeling special feels fucking fantastic. Bangkok: happy, peaceful, joyous, whole. Not special — human. Being served with pride and excellence vs. treated as a useless idiot in Australia. Never wanted special. This is being allowed to just BE human — and it's the best thing.

3. The meds raised the floor — they didn't change the physics. First dose: a peace and lack of racing ADHD anxiety I'd never felt — the exact effect I'd been manufacturing myself via supplements and self-guided chemistry (I do it well, but at real cost). What they did NOT touch: I still need regulation, grounding (eat, train, hydrate), and not overdoing the work. A bad environment used to make a −5; now a −1/−2. Same pattern, far shallower floor. Handle = habits, not worry.

4. Laura mirrored my entire identity back as her own — and I didn't reach for the scoreboard. She stated her values for the first time ever — pay-it-forward, build-value, into-the-sunset, business/podcast — my words, my mirrors, framed as her own, with zero acknowledgment it's what I've invited her into for years. Old me points it out: &quotI've been telling you this for years, ask anyone who's known me.&quot I didn't. Just happy for her, proud of her. Not even telling her. That's the covert contract dead on the table — vote-for-me landing at the deepest level it ever has. Lived, not concept.

5. The fix-it wore a new costume — and I caught it. A blow-up ~a week ago: I was Teaching/fixing Laura — all the reasons her external-attacking behaviour pushed away the very people she wanted. Clear to me, still is. But that was the covert fix-it-for-me format in a new suit. I voted for me instead: communicated what I wanted, what I didn't, what I felt. No scoreboard. Things landed wildly more aligned — basically no good, no bad.

6. The body was closed and old news. Pelvic floor: a near-non-event a month or two before leaving Melbourne — the shed, ~3L in, 4 hours, good before/during/after, because good inside. Talked about openly with Laura and repeatedly with Tank. Never the issue — a symptom of misalignment. Good inside → good bladder. That loop is closed. Whatever was out of alignment clearly isn't anymore.

7. The Caleb fire is lit, wanted, and watched — not built. Alive and authentic a week and a half. Done more in that direction here than years would have in Australia. Watching is enough for now — no specific outcome yet. Two wants, written in the Caleb journal: (1) get Section 8 investing + income functioning as it should — more income, stability, certainty, because I want that for me; (2) be around people more like me (Caleb = example) — be in aligned rooms, not the one dragging the room off.

8. I don't feel alone — and I don't need to be understood the way I used to. Always ahead-of-the-curve, always felt a bit alone at times — but I don't feel remotely alone or lonely now.
